npc_steward.title: Steward Wren Ashby
lines: "I was polishing silver in the cellar all evening," he insists, hands trembling.
"I wasn't doing nothing in the Barn, so if you saw me over there it wasn't me!"

npc_voss.title: Commodore Brannigan Voss
lines: "I heard the toast from the watch post," Voss says, a little too quickly.
"Ask anyone. I've nothing to hide." He stares at you intently as he says this.

npc_kestrel.title: Merchant Odalys Kestrel
lines: "I never even spoke to Lord Duskmere tonight," Kestrel claims, arms folded.
"I was working all evening until they called us over here. Go on and check the dock office if you don't believe me."

npc_marrow.title: Architect Tobias Marrow
lines: "I was proud to have played my part in rebuilding these walls," Marrow says, gesturing at the estate house.
"Every stone on schedule, a-and to the letter of the contract." He doesn't quite meet your eyes when he says it.

npc_ashgate.title: Lady Isobel Ashgate
lines: She regards you coolly over the rim of her glass, entirely unbothered by the commotion.

"A dreadful business. Alaric and I had our disagreements, of course, land mostly, tedious inheritance laws, but murder? I have people for that sort of thing. I certainly don't attend to it myself." She smiles, and gives you absolutely nothing else.

npc_corwin.title: Silas "Hook" Corwin
lines: He looks you up and down, then leans in close.
"You lot look like you're about to get blamed for somethin' you didn't do. "
"If you want to know more about Marrow, he's left a survey marker out near the river. Always thought that was odd."

npc_nell.title: Nell Cobb
lines: "Didn't expect to see Champions of Azeroth at a fancy garden party!"
"Free drinks though, can't complain. Shame about the poisoning. Really put a damper on the evening."
"Between us? I saw the Commodore down by the river a few nights back. Didn't think much of it at the time, but now murder? Suspect if you ask me."

npc_reyes.title: Sergeant Reyes
lines: The watch sergeant won't quite meet your eyes. "I was on my rounds. The whole time. "
He keeps glancing over the bridge and looking back towards the manor.

npc_maid.title: A Lady's Maid
lines: She's refilling glasses that don't need refilling, doing her best to look busy.
"I've been working the whole evening, sir. Haven't left the courtyard once." Her hands are shaking, and she keeps glancing toward the farm animals.
\Then again, maybe she just doesn't like cows?

npc_guest_a1.title: A Gala Guest
lines: "Terrible thing, isn't it? Though between us, Duskmere's wine cellar is still the best. Shame to let it go to waste tonight."

npc_guest_a2.title: A Gala Guest
lines: "I heard the Commodore wanted this whole gala moved up a week. Odd, that. Probably nothing."

npc_guest_b1.title: A Gala Guest
lines: "Champions of Azeroth, here, at a garden party! My cousin fought at the Shrine, you know. Never shuts up about it."

npc_guest_b2.title: A Gala Guest
lines: "I do hope they let us go home soon. My feet are absolutely killing me in these shoes."

npc_guest_a3.title: A Gala Guest
lines: "Surprised to see Lady Ashgate still here - I heard the engagement was called off awfully suddenly..."

npc_guest_c1.title: A Gala Guest
lines: "Lady Ashgate looked awfully calm when they found the body, don't you think? ...No, I'm sure that means nothing."

npc_guest_c2.title: A Gala Guest
lines: "Best gala Boralus has seen since the Siege, and now this. Poor Alaric. Poor seating chart, honestly. Who's going to redo it?"

doc_ashby.title: The Repair Ledger
doc_ashby.intro: A ledger of seven repair-fund withdrawals sits beside a small tin of numbered receipt stubs.
list: Stub 401 — Coastwood Timber, 40g
Stub 402 — Coastwood Timber, 25g
Stub 403 — Ironbell Smithy, 15g
Stub 404 — Harrow & Vale, 90g
Stub 405 — Coastwood Timber, 55g
Stub 406 — Ironbell Smithy, 20g
doc_ashby.closing: Six stubs for seven withdrawals. If they were issued in ledger order, simply skipping whichever entry lacked a receipt, stub 404 ought to belong to the fifth withdrawal, the Coastwood Timber roof beams. Instead it's stamped for Harrow & Vale, the seventh. Something's been renumbered to cover a gap. The fourteenth-of-Wanning withdrawal, paid straight to 'Duskmere Estate' itself for a contingency reserve, has no stub at all.

doc_voss.title: The Burnt Satchel
doc_voss.intro: Half-buried among the reeds, a satchel holds five torn, part-burned betting slips. None show a full date, only fragments of context.
list: "...paid in full after the regatta, no hard feelings, B.V." (5g)
"...third marker this month, Voss, I'm done extending you credit" (20g)
"...since the Siege repairs started, you've been good for it, but this one's steep" (35g)
"...if this isn't settled by the gala I'm bringing it to Thorne myself" (80g)
"...he swears the ship's coming in any day now. It never does." (a side note, different hand, no amount)
doc_voss.closing: The regatta came before the Siege repairs began. Read in that order, the debt only grows, and the largest, most recent slip threatens to go straight to Thorne by the night of the gala.

doc_kestrel.title: The Shipping Manifests
doc_kestrel.intro: A locked dock office, and inside, a stack of shipping manifests.
doc_kestrel.closing: Two of these describe the same ship, Providence's Grace, departing and arriving in the same instant, twice. No ship sails that fast. Something was filed on paper that never needed to happen on water.

doc_marrow.title: The Blueprint Tube
doc_marrow.intro: Buried near the survey marker, a tube holding two documents: the original contracted repairs, and a second sheet recording what actually got built.
doc_marrow.closing: Two structures exist on the grounds that never appeared on the original contract. Marrow built them anyway, and was never paid for the overage.

doc_ashgate.title: Torn Correspondence
doc_ashgate.intro: A locked travelling chest holds a letter torn into six pieces, mixed in with a shawl-mending receipt and a dinner invitation that don't matter at all.
list: "...the solicitor confirms House Ashgate's holdings will not..."
"...cover the debts by autumn. If the engagement proceeds as..."
"...planned, everything changes. I am told the..."
"...paperwork can be finalized within the month, well before..."
"...the anniversary of the Siege. I cannot say this..."
"...to anyone, least of all him, until it is settled..."
doc_ashgate.closing: Pieced together in order, the letter reads: 'The solicitor confirms House Ashgate's holdings will not cover the debts by autumn. If the engagement proceeds as planned, everything changes. I am told the paperwork can be finalized within the month, well before the anniversary of the Siege. I cannot say this to anyone, least of all him, until it is settled.' Weeks before the gala, and already a matter of real urgency.

doc_reyes.title: The Patrol Rota
doc_reyes.intro: Folded inside a broken lantern near the bridge: a watch schedule, in Boralus City Watch bells rather than plain hours.
list: First Bell, dusk
Second Bell, one candle-mark after dusk
Third Bell, two candle-marks after dusk — Reyes
Fourth Bell, midnight
Fifth Bell, two candle-marks after midnight
doc_reyes.closing: A second note, half-torn: 'R. asked to swap Third for Fourth, said he'd cover the gap himself, told no one.' A crossed-out watch log shows someone else covering Fourth Bell instead. Reyes' actual post, Third Bell, two candle-marks after dusk, was empty.

doc_maid.title: The Hidden Diary
doc_maid.intro: Buried beneath a loose stone in the kitchen garden: a diary, half in plain hand, half in a simple lover's code.
doc_maid.closing: Decoded, one entry reads: 'He waits for me where the old boathouse used to stand, past the willow, where no one from the house ever walks after dark.' Whoever she's meeting, she's gone to real trouble to avoid naming him.

evidence_table.title: The Evidence Table
evidence_table.description: Bring every piece of evidence you're carrying and add it to the table.
